title: Composition for tin or tin alloy electroplating comprising suppressing agent
abstract: An aqueous composition comprising tin ions and at least one compound of formula (I) wherein X 1 , X 2 are independently selected from a linear or branched C 1 -C 12 alkanediyl, which may optionally be interrupted by O or S, R 11 is a monovalent group of formula â(O-CH 2 -CHR 41 ) m -OR 42 ,R 12 , R 13 , R 14 are independently selected from H, R 11 , and R 40 ; R 15 is selected from H, R 11 , R 40 and -X 4 -N(R 21 ) 2 , X 4 is a divalent group selected from (a) a linear or branched C 1 to C 12 alkanediyl, and (b) formula â(O-CH 2 -CHR 41 )o-, R 21 is selected from R 11 and R 40 , R 40 is a linear or branched C 1 -C 20 alkyl, R 41 is selected from H and a linear or branched C 1 to C 5 alkyl, R 42 is selected from H and a linear or branched C 1 -C 20 alkyl, which may optionally be substituted by hydroxy, alkoxy or alkoxycarbonyl, n is an integer of from 1 to 6, 2 m is an integer of from 2 to 250, and o is an integer of from 1 to 250.
claims: Claims Claims An aqueous composition comprising tin ions and at least one compound of formula I wherein X 1 , X 2 are independently selected from a linear or branched C1-C12 alkanediyl, which may optionally be interrupted by O or S, R 11 is a monovalent group of formula -(0-CH 2 -CHR 41 ) m -0R 42 , R 12 , R 13 , R 14 are independently selected from H, R 11 , and R 40 ; R 15 is selected from H, R 11 , R 40 and -X 4 -N(R 21 ) 2 , X 4 is a divalent group selected from (a) a linear or branched C1 to C12 alkanediyl, and (b) formula -(0-CH 2 -CHR 41 ) 0 -, R 21 is selected from R 11 and R 40 , R 40 is a linear or branched C1-C20 alkyl, R 41 is selected from H and a linear or branched C1 to C5 alkyl, R 42 is selected from H and a linear or branched C1-C20 alkyl, which may optionally be substituted by hydroxy, alkoxy or alkoxycarbonyl, n is an integer of from 1 to 6, m is an integer of from 2 to 250, and o is an integer of from 1 to 250. The aqueous composition according to claim 1 , wherein X 1 and X 2 are independently selected from a C1-C6 alkanediyl, preferably methanediyl, ethanediyl or propanediyl. The aqueous composition according to claim 1 , wherein X 1 and X 2 are -(CHR 41 ) q -[Q- (CHR 41 ) r ] s -, wherein Q is selected from O, S wherein q + r s is the number of C atoms in the spacer. 4. An aqueous composition according to claim 3, wherein Q=0 and q=r = 1 or 2. 5. The aqueous composition according to anyone of the preceding claims, wherein R 41 is selected from H, methyl and ethyl, preferably H and methyl. The aqueous composition according to anyone of the preceding claims, wherein R 12 , R 13 and R 14 are selected from R 11 . 7. The aqueous composition according to anyone of the preceding claims, wherein R 15 is selected from R 11 and -X 4 -N(R 21 ) 2 . 8. The aqueous composition according to anyone of the preceding claims, wherein R 11 is a copolymer of ethylene oxide and a further C 3 to C 4 alkylene oxide. 9. The aqueous composition according to claims 8, wherein the content of ethylene oxide in the copolymer of ethylene oxide and the further C3 to C 4 alkylene oxide is from 5 to 50 % by weight, preferably 5 to 40 % by weight. 10. The aqueous composition according to claims 8, wherein the content of ethylene oxide in the copolymer of ethylene oxide and the further C3 to C 4 alkylene oxide is from 5 to 30 % by weight, preferably from 8 to 20 % by weight. 1 1. The aqueous composition according to anyone of the preceding claims, which comprises a single grain refiner that is no a,b-unsaturated aliphatic carbonyl compound. 12. The aqueous composition according to anyone of the preceding claims, which comprises essentially no grain refiner. 13. Use of the aqueous composition according to anyone of the preceding claims for depositing tin or tin alloys on a substrate comprising features having an aperture size from 500 nm to 500 pm. 14. A process for electrodepositing tin or a tin alloy onto a substrate by a) contacting a composition according to anyone of claims 1 to 12 with the substrate, and b) applying a current to the substrate for a time sufficient to deposit a tin or tin alloy layer onto the substrate, wherein the substrate comprises features having an aperture size from 500 nm to 500 pm and the deposition is performed to fill these features. 15 The process according to claims 14, wherein the aperture size is from 1 pm to 200 pm.
